target_endpoints.description: A list of targeting objects. Target objects specify FQDN and/or serial number values. The deployment will be targeted to endpoints that match against the targeting values. If FQDN and serial number are provided then the endpoint must match against both FQDN and serial number to be targeted. If only FQDN or serial number is provided then endpoints that match against the provided value will be targeted by the deployment. (If provided, this array must be non-empty.)

patch_tanium_uids.description: Tanium UIDs of the patches to include in the deployment. (The length of this array must at most 50.)

patch_operation.description: Patch operation. If osType is "LINUX" then patch_operation cannot be "UNINSTALL".

version.description: The version of the patch list that will be used. (If version is not provided, then the latest version of the patch list will be used.)

patch_lists.description: A list of patch-list objects. If patch_tanium_uids is provided, then this field is optional.

target_limiting_group_ids.description: A list of computer group IDs to restrict deployment to. The All Computers group will be used if this field is not provided.

start_time.description: Start time of deployment. (In epoch UTC milliseconds; must be no earlier than current time.)

end_time.description: End time of deployment. (In epoch UTC milliseconds; must be no earlier than start_time.)

distribute_over_time.description: The number of minutes to distribute the deployment over. (When set to 0, disables DoT; when not set, automatically selects a probably-correct value based on the deployment length. Must be no longer than the total length of the deployment.)

override_block_list.description: Override patch block lists? To set this to true, user needs either: Patch Deployment Write OR (Patch Deployment Block List Override Write AND Patch Deployment Override Restricted Write). (Defaults to false.)
